data.title: As a developer, project-setup tests target onboarding SKILL.md with behavioral assertions
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the 4 bash test files (test-project-setup-*.sh) and 1 Python test file (test_project_setup_ci_autodetection.py with 7 test functions) to read onboarding/SKILL.md instead of project-setup/SKILL.md. Add behavioral assertions that verify SC1-SC5 content exists in onboarding SKILL.md.
**Why**: These tests still reference the project-setup redirect stub, causing CI failures. After stories 1-4 add the required content to onboarding SKILL.md, these tests should pass when retargeted.
**Scope**:
- IN: Retarget 4 bash test files + 1 Python test file (7 functions) to onboarding/SKILL.md; add behavioral assertions for CI keys (SC1), workflow selection (SC2), deprecation migration (SC3), version disambiguation (SC4), key name corrections (SC5)
- OUT: Writing new test files; changing test-redirect-stubs.sh, test-sub-agent-guard.sh, test-dso-setup.sh, or test-gh-identity-resolver.sh (these reference project-setup for other reasons)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all 4 bash test files and all 7 Python test functions read onboarding/SKILL.md instead of project-setup/SKILL.md
  ← Satisfies: "SC6 — test retargeting"
- When this story is complete, CI Python Skills workflow passes with 0 failures
  ← Satisfies: "SC6 — CI green"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] The 7 Python test functions assert CI keys exist in SKILL.md — they will only pass after story 1 adds those keys
- [Testing] Tests must include behavioral assertions (not just file-path updates) — at least some tests should exercise SC1-SC5 behavior
